title: DeathSentence
description: A very versatile death message plugin with sensible fallbacks. From adding your own custom death messages to reformatting the vanilla ones, this plugin has got you covered!
body:
# Death Sentence

## Introduction

DeathSentence is a plugin that allows you to customize death messages. The configuration provides extensive freedom to specify death messages for very specific circumstances. For example you could create a death message that you only get if a snowman kills you by throwing a snowball at an end crystal, which you explode from. Additionally, you could specify that this message should only appear for players with a certain permission, for players in a certain type of world as well as several other options!

The default configuration comes with a wide range of death messages for all of the circumstances in which a player regularly dies in the vanilla game. It also contains a lot of comments that explain how to configure the plugin.

## Feature overview

- Specify death messages for both very broad and very specific circumstances
- Support for custom text formatting (MiniMessage)
- PlaceholderAPI support
- Customize DeathMessage formatting (this includes vanilla messages)
- Configurable cooldown between death messages for a player
- Advanced fallback logic - if none of the specific death messages apply a more generic one is used (you will always get a death message)
- Allows for keeping the vanilla death messages and only expanding/reformatting them

## Installation

To install the plugin, simply Download the ``.jar``-File and drop it into your ``plugins`` Folder. Once you restart the server, a plugin folder (``plugins/DeathSentence``) will appear, in which you can find the default ``config.yml``. In there you can find detailed instructions on how to configure the plugin.

## Support

If you encounter any issues with this plugin, you can either submit a bug report via [GitHub](https://github.com/SlimeNexus/deathsentence/issues/) or if you have general questions you can write in ``#support`` on our [Discord Server](https://slime.nexus/discord/).
